transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hello.
How are you?
I'm good.
I'm just tired, so I got another cup of coffee.
How are you?
Can't complain.
I saw that someone, oh, Josh.
Yeah.
Is he new?
No, Josh.
Hi, Josh.
I'll let you explain.
Yeah.
I would just get to the audio.
What?
What?
Yeah, I saw that you were joining our calls, so I was just, yeah, curious about what you were doing at Navan.
Yeah, sure.
And hello.
Yeah, I joined Navan in late November on the product marketing team, and I'm specifically focused on the expense and implement solutions.
Oh, cool.
Great to meet you.
Yeah, you as well.
Yeah, I'm Aida.
I am a content strategist slash engagement manager at GrowthX.
So I'm basically your main point of contact during this 12-week sprint process.
Navan is currently in week 10 out of 12.
So the purpose of the sprint is really to develop.
We have a content generation platform that uses AI and all sorts of different LLMs to create blog content.
And during the 12-week sprint, we, like, generate the foundational inputs, like the company.

company context, writing guidelines, products and services matrix that we input into the platform, and then we start generating blog content, which we've been doing consistently for the past couple of weeks.
I think what you'd be most interested in, I added you to our notion, but we have a products and services matrix that plays a pretty big role in how we talk about Navan in our content.

So anything, you know, when you get the chance, it would be cool if you could take a look at it and let us know if something's off, or we could be highlighting, like, additional features or benefits that you think would be a really good value add.
Great.
Is that the one in Airtable, the content OS?
Oh, that's our content calendar.
So let me share my screen.
Just for context, I'm able to stay for 15 minutes, and I wanted to specifically talk about, like, Navan, AI, SEO, page.
Pages or a few pages we were looking at, just a level set on my timing and scope for today, but yeah, feel free to, I would love to see what you're referring to.
Cool.
So yeah, really quickly, so this is the Notion space that I added you to.
We have the performance report linked here, the Looker dashboard that I added you to, all of our meetings are here, and then the docs are here.
And then we have all of like the different artifacts here, and this is the products and services matrix that I was talking about.

And yeah, we are, we were discussing sort of the AI messaging over the past couple of weeks, because we do, in the content calendar, we have like a bunch of topics that are related to AI, so I would love to hear like any info from you that you have right now.
Yeah, great.
Yeah, and for context, so two of the things I'm working on, one of which is a Nervon intelligence webpage, that's going to go on our main app of our marketing site.
Working on the contents of that, a lot of the inputs are coming directly from our E&E AI messaging guide.

As a subset, or I guess using actually a lot of what's included there, we're creating some SDO pages, aka some blogs from you guys, and I was working on scoping those out, using some of the same inputs from the AI messaging guide.

So what I've started to put together is an outline of those, and I was hoping to just better understand next steps for making sure you guys have the right inputs, and that it is possible to create some of these over the coming weeks.
The intelligence page, the SCR page, is all part of a Navan C&E AI campaign that's launching in February.
Okay, got it.
So the AI messaging, is that good to go, and can that be shared with us?
Yes.
And I guess maybe a question for you and Nima and the others.
Has the AI team messaging guide doc been shared with growthx?
I know I was slacking Libby about it, but I wasn't sure if that was actually shared with you guys.
Does anybody know?
Because I haven't received it, so probably I think you can just shoot it over in our external channel.
You're referring to the AI messaging?
Yeah, AI product messaging, T&E.
Okay.
There was one that Libby shared, so let me double check.
Was it this one?
Let me double check.
So this one?
So I would think of this as like the atom.
Yeah.
It's
It shared in the channel on January 13th.
Okay.
So I think you guys will have this then, Ada.
Okay.
Okay.
I will double check.
We saw a slide deck.
Okay.
Yeah.
And you can think of this as like the atom that's been sort of built up into the slide diff.
It's a lot of the language here is a direct input to the slide deck.
A lot of the language here is a direct input to the intelligence webpage, and it is also an input for some of the SEO pages.

So what I did is take some of the language here and then start to draft out some brainstorming of the type of, based on topic cluster, some of the potential questions or agents that are mentioned in the product messaging, and then some of the potential like punchlines, just trying to give you guys some inputs to use beyond that messaging guide.
what be I did did is is Thank you.
The deck, and then Amaran shared some keywords as well.
I tried to list those as potential inputs, but wasn't sure what you guys typically require for this type of request.
Okay, great.
So this is good.
So the messaging is key here.
We have, you have access to our content OS.
So right now we have 16 topics in the AI cluster.

However, what I want to do is take this messaging doc and do additional keyword research to just further boost this cluster, because a lot of it is related, like a lot of the content is more top and middle of the funnel.
I think we could go further, like lower down the funnel.
But again, I would like to also understand like the purpose of this campaign.
Is it like, are you trying to just like, like raise awareness?
Are you trying to like drive it?
Is it specific pages or like demo requests?
That's also helpful for me to know.

I'll let Nima speak to this, but my two cents is for the SEO pages, the main goal is driving awareness and linking each of them to the Navan Intelligence page as a way to funnel people back to that central source.

So it's a little less on bottom of funnel, like long guides and book and demos, and more on shorter, very SEO-friendly featuring a fourth problem from some of our team diapersonics and getting those folks over to the Intelligence page to learn more.
But I'll let Nima clarify.
Yeah, they could be short or long, top or bottom of funnel.
I'm not as worried about it, but 100% agree with Josh.
Josh, they all have to funnel back people to the Intelligence page.
Okay.
Yeah, I'll just pop this up as the goals.
Got it.
No, that sounds good to me.
Yep.
We also have two other persona blogs here, Aida.
This one's already published.
This one's being published tomorrow.
Okay.
And I would just say those are other links that we could direct people to as the contents of this will likely be specific AI functionality or agents that are described in one of these blogs.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
All right.
I think we're already working on content for this week, but what we can do this week is I will do additional keyword research to find additional topics for this cluster.
And then for next week, I can send you, I will try to do that tomorrow or Thursday.
I will send you a list of five topics that I recommend getting started.

We will use the AI messaging to update all of the artifacts that we're using for content and generation and I will let our editor know to read and like use the two blogs that you published like AI for Finance and AI for Travel Managers to also like read them and just incorporate any important elements into the blogs that we're working on just to make sure that there are no discrepancies between the different blogs and how we're talking about.
these solutions.
Aida, you also have...
Sorry, Josh.
You also have like a link to a keyword research I did back then on AI-related keywords that you can use from the doc.
Pretty extensive.
Perfect.
Thank you.
All right.
That sounds good.
When is the launch of the new AI?
We're shooting for, I have to double confirm this.
Is it February 9th?
I've got to confirm.
Okay.
But it's the second week of February.
Okay.
Sounds good.
So I think you guys added me to this channel, but I can list the link to the SEO, like brainstorming with the link to the other inputs, as well as the...

Link that we will use for the intelligence page webpage, which again isn't live, as well as a link to the AI finance blog, which is live, and then placeholder for the travel manager's one that's coming in the next day or two.
Okay.
Okay.
That sounds good.
All right.
Yeah.
I'm pretty clear on like what the next steps are here.
So I will just keep you posted in the external channel.
And if anything changes or if you have any additional ideas or inputs, you can always just...
I will will will will just...
Send a message in our shared Slack.
Yeah, sounds good.
Appreciate it.
Nice to meet you.
And Nima, Rich, Moran, anything I'm missing just from executing this?
No, the only thing is that as the material comes in, let us know if you want to be part of the editorial for at least the first one, but usually Rich and Libby own that piece.
But let us know if you want to be involved.
Okay.
I think maybe it'll check the first one out, but I mean, I trust you guys and you have the context and other assets to make sure that us insistent.
So I think it should be fun.
Cool.
Sounds good.
All right, cool.
I got a job, but thanks, guys.
Take care.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
All right.
Great.
I shared our agenda over email, but I'm going to go ahead and share my screen.
and I'll go share screen.
I saw that you were in the Looker dashboard, and you had some questions about the cohort report.

So I thought that maybe we could jump into that, and I could just show you really quickly how it works, and then we could get into our AI visibility platform, unless you have any other questions or anything else you want to discuss first.
Sounds good.
Let's start with that.
Thanks.
Awesome.
So this is the Looker dashboard, and you should all have editing access, which kind of allows you to manipulate the data here.
So what this page does is combines data from Google Search Console and Google Analytics 4.
One of the key inputs here are the URL cohorts.
So whenever you publish one of our blog posts, we go into the data sources, and we add the publish links to the backend, and we also assign.
Each URL to the topic cluster it belongs to, which allows us to track the performance of individual clusters.
Everything else on your website that we did not publish is categorized as non-growthx URLs.
And you can just simply uncheck this to filter that traffic out.
So here you can see the overall sessions week over week for the content that we've published so far.
I know that Libby published four new blogs yesterday or perhaps late last week.
So I just need to go in and add those URLs here as well.
Another thing that you can do is adjust the session medium here.
So if you're only interested in referral traffic, can see referral later.
We might start seeing also traffic from social media, email.
So you're also able to filter different traffic.
And it's important to note that this chart right here is pulled from Google Analytics 4.
And this table right here as well is also pulled from Google Analytics 4, which allows us to see the overall engagement rate on different pages, which is the exact opposite of bounce rate.
So a page that an engaged session counts as a session that lasts longer than 10 seconds, or it includes like the user clicked on a link on the page or the user scrolled on the page.
So that counts.
All of those three things count as engaged sessions.
And then this table right here just shows the data from Google Search Console, which is data on organic search traffic from Google specifically.
So we can see how many clicks different pages have gotten.
So far, how many impressions, what's the average click-through rate, and what's the average position for all of the keywords that Google has detected for a specific URL?
So do you have any questions around how we track this or anything else?
Got it.
So I was looking at the all traffic.
That was my bad.
So two questions.
Well, does it show the keywords, you said, or just the URL?
It shows just the URLs.
If you want to look, if we want to look at the keywords, we can go into your Google Search Console and then extract, like, what are the keywords that you're ranking for?
And we can also do that in SEMrush position tracking.
So we can notice that.
Yeah, that would be great.
And then also, the thing is that I don't know if these numbers are good or bad.
Obviously, don't look huge, but it could be just a matter of time.
of time.
Here let's go.
don't know.
So long tail approach that you might be taking here, but what good looks like?
Yeah, so right now what we're looking at in the first, considering we published our first content two weeks ago, this is like the signals are very positive.
We are targeting like more long tail, lower volume keywords here.
But what we're looking at in the first couple of weeks is did the content get indexed, meaning is it Google serving it up in search results, which is happening here?
What are the keywords that it's ranking for?
And is it like what's the overall click-through rate?
But our approach really is to try to scale publishing like week over week.
So with consistent publishing, five per week, we can really see what's working and what isn't working.
And another thing that's missing right now from the analysis is just all of the
The competitive blogs that we've created so far that are with the legal team who's reviewing them, those blogs really make a big impact both on AI visibility and also in terms of clicks.
So we would love for just to be able to get them approved as quickly as possible so that we can publish them.
But compared to all of the customers, all of the clients that we've worked with so far, this is on par with what we usually see.
So it's steady week over week growth.
And these are the typical numbers that we see.
But we do try to publish very consistently like week over week so that each week is building on the next.
So when should we expect a critical mass of traffic coming in?
So if we start publishing.
So five blogs every week.
We typically within six weeks, we start to see like traffic really ramping up.

I can share some, I don't have the numbers in front of me, but I can share some case studies with you that we have from other clients so that you can kind of see what, so you can kind of like visualize what to expect.
Okay.
Yeah.
All right.
And then last week, I mentioned to both Rich and Libby that our GrowthX has built our own AI visibility tracking platform called Check That.
And we've already set up your workspace here.
Just to call out right away, this dip that you're seeing, it's not because anything's wrong.
I just added more prompts that are more middle of the funnel, where you're not that visible.
It's
You are very visible in bottom of the funnel prompts where we are asking about very specific tools or comparing alternatives to other tools.
But when it comes to some middle of the funnel queries, you're not that visible.
But to step back a little bit, so check that is, like I said, our AI visibility platform where we measure all of our clients' AI visibility in these different LLMs.
And check that is a part of your contract, so it's not going to cost you anything extra.
Right now, access is just limited to GrowthX, but we are actively building the capability of sharing access with you.
So soon you will also have access to this dashboard.
You'll be able to go in, click through different prompts, and measure and export all the data.
And another thing that makes check that different to other AI visibility platforms is that it's a shared...

index of AI visibility, meaning that we have already built profiles for, I think, over 1,500 different companies across different categories, and we're already tracking over 100,000 prompts for all of those companies so that whenever someone signs up, they already have months of data to use.
For example, like all of this data that goes back from before January 15th, this is all data that comes from the prompts that we were already tracking and tracking for all of your competitors.
Right now, Navan is in the expense and travel category, and these are all of the competitors that we are monitoring in this category with, like, their own prompts.
So that's kind of, and the prompts that we're targeting in our shared index are all.
All evaluation stage prompts.
So they are mostly bottom of the funnel prompts that directly influence buying decisions.
To give you a little bit of a tour.
So we already have your context here.
So we've added the Navan company context here.
We've added the product and features matrix, as well as all of the personas.
This influences the competitors that are being actively recommended in the platform.
And it also influences, like, the kind of prompts that we might suggest.
These are the competitors that we're actively, that I actively am tracking here.
But we can also add any additional competitors here, as well.
So these are the ones that are, that feature on your website.
So there's, like, a versus alternatives page.
So I selected them from here.
So there, but we can add additional ones as needed.
And then these are the prompts that we're tracking.
So right now we're tracking 201 prompts in different stages.
And we can kind of see what your overall visibility and citation rate is for each of these prompts.
So for example, are there platforms that integrate travel bookings with expense management?
We can click through.
And we can see how your overall visibility has changed for that prompt.
And we can also see what are all the other brands that are consistently appearing in these citations.
And we can also see, okay, for different dates, did you appear, did you not appear?
Aida, you be able to share the prompts with me?
Yes, yes, absolutely.
I can share them with you.
So two things.
One, do we see.
see.
Do the volume of these prompts?
Yeah, so that's unfortunately, you mean the volume of prompts in like chatGPD, like how many people are asking this?
Yeah, the visibility is great, but if nobody is maybe wanting to bloom on somebody's searches for it, why does it matter?
That's one question.
And the other part of it is that if the volume is high, what stops us from creating custom content dedicated to those prompts?
Yeah, great question.
Unfortunately, none of these LLMs actually share data around how many people are searching for these prompts.
So what we do is we use the audience personas, we use your company context to try to craft prompts that would translate to reasonable search behavior in your audience.
So we know know from just a
Talking to different people in the advisory community from our contacts at Hypergrowth that a lot of decision makers are using different LLMs to research different solutions.
I'm talking to clients every week and I'm hearing more and more people come to us saying, oh, like someone found us on ChatGPT or someone found us on Perplexity.
So it is, Google is a dominant search player and it's still certainly very much relevant, but we also want to make sure that we're monitoring different prompts that Navan could appear in.
And you'll notice the prompts that we're tracking, they're very, we try to keep them simple, you know, like what's better, Agencia or Navan?
Or like, are there platforms that integrate travel bookings with expense management?
So we're, we do our best not to lead the witness, meaning to create.
Prompts that are so specific that only Navan would show up.
We really want to make sure that the prompts are something that one of your ICPs would type into ChatGPT or Perplexity.
And then another important part of the platform are the sources.
So this source view allows us to see what are the URLs that are being cited the most.
And this helps us inform our content strategy.
So, for example, one of the top cited articles is five best travel and expense management software by Tipalti.
We're not tracking them as a competitor, but it's still going to show up here because it appears consistently in the prompt responses.
And a lot of the sources are citing these types of blog posts that target this keyword travel and expense.
Management Software, which we have noticed Navan doesn't have a dedicated blog post for.
One of the blog posts that we actually delivered last week targets this exact keyword.
So this is an important content gap for us to cover with the blog content.
And yeah, I mean, this kind of list, we can like go back in time and we can also search through different sources to find different competitors.
So this allows us to like identify any articles from competitors that are being surfaced really well and we can kind of steal their visibility.
Any other questions that I can help with?
Okay, I will share the list of prompts in.
In Slack, and then you can let me know if there's anything else that you want us to add so we can continue adding prompts to this list as needed.
When do you think we will have access?
I have to check with the team this week.
We didn't have a hard date last week, but it's such a huge priority internally to get our customers access because everybody wants access.
So it's a huge, huge priority that is actively being developed.
So I'll let you know.
All right.
And then other than that, I think the other content updates that we have, I can share them in our external channel.
I know Libby and Rich have been reviewing the drafts that we submitted both last week and the week prior.
So we will resolve any of that.
Feedback and update the status, second draft review.
And I will also update the published blogs with any new content that we published recently.
Quick question for Rich.
When I go on your blog, are there any blog posts that don't appear on the main index page?
Like, do you hide any blogs?
We do.
Okay.
Okay.
Got it.
So, just so I can use the URL slug to find the blogs that we published recently.
Correct.
All right.
So, I know we're at time.
So, I think those are all the updates that I had.
Is there anything else that you wanted to discuss today?
Did you wear your hat for one thing?
Yeah.
Nope.
All right.
Got it.
Thank you all so much.
I will send a follow-up in Slack.
Thank you.
Thank
And yeah, I'll see you next week.
Thank you.
Thanks, Aida.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on the AI content strategy for the upcoming campaign.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**AI Content Strategy:** A new content cluster will support the February AI campaign. The strategy is awareness-focused, using short-form SEO blogs to funnel traffic to the central "Navan Intelligence" page.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
  - [**Messaging Source:** The "T\&E AI Product Messaging" doc is the single source of truth for all campaign content, ensuring consistent language and positioning.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Performance Baseline:** Early Looker data shows positive indexing and ranking signals. Aida will share case studies to set expectations for the typical 6-week ramp-up period for new content.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)
  - [**AI Visibility Insights:** The "Check That" platform reveals a key content gap: Navan lacks a dedicated blog for the high-value keyword "travel and expense management software," which is frequently cited by LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1575.0)

## Topics

### AI Content Strategy & Campaign Support

  - [Josh Trach (PMM, Expense & Implement) joined to align on content for the "Navan C\&E AI campaign," launching in the second week of February.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [**Campaign Goal:** Drive awareness and funnel traffic to the central "Navan Intelligence" webpage.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
  - [**Content Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
      - [Create short, SEO-friendly blogs.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
      - [Link all blogs to the "Navan Intelligence" page.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
      - [Repurpose content from existing persona blogs ("AI for Finance," "AI for Travel Managers") to maintain consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
  - [**Messaging Source:** The "T\&E AI Product Messaging" doc is the definitive source for all campaign language.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Inputs:** Aida will use the messaging doc and Moran's existing AI keyword research to generate new topics.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=448.0)

### Content Performance & Looker Dashboard

  - [Aida reviewed the Looker dashboard, which combines GA4 and GSC data to track content performance.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [**Key Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
      - [**GA4:** Sessions, engagement rate (opposite of bounce rate).](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
      - [**GSC:** Clicks, impressions, CTR, average keyword position.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=942.0)
  - [**Performance Context:**](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)
      - [Early data shows positive indexing and ranking signals.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1026.0)
      - [Traffic ramps up after \~6 weeks of consistent publishing (5 blogs/week).](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1131.0)
      - [**Blocker:** Competitive blogs, which drive significant traffic, are awaiting legal approval.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1072.0)
  - [**Keyword Data:** The dashboard shows URL performance, not individual keywords. Aida can pull keyword data from SEMrush or GSC on request.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=984.0)

### AI Visibility with "Check That" Platform

  - [Aida demoed "Check That," GrowthX's platform for tracking AI visibility in LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity).](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
  - [**Platform Features:**](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1214.0)
      - [**Competitive Benchmarking:** Tracks 1,500+ companies and 100k+ prompts, providing historical data.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1253.0)
      - [**Prompt Analysis:** Tracks visibility and citation rate for 201 prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1385.0)
      - [**Source Identification:** Reveals top-cited URLs, identifying content gaps.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1575.0)
  - [**Key Finding:** Navan lacks a dedicated blog for "travel and expense management software," a keyword frequently cited by LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1575.0)
  - [**Access:** Currently GrowthX-only; client access is a high-priority feature in development.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=1214.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=574.0)
      - [Conduct keyword research using the AI messaging doc and Moran's list.](https://fathom.video/share/gz_Gcn8VUbQe1ZQZJtsDcoseR6xXd_Ti?tab=summary&timestamp=448.0)
